ActoinsPriceExplanation:
In this route you will be charged 29 NIS in advance and you can perform up to 100 operations without additional payment, over 100 operations you will be charged another 30 NIS and you can perform another 150 additional operations at no cost (a total of 250 operations - 59 NIS), at the end of this amount of operations You will be charged NIS 60 again and you will be entitled to another 250 actions (a total of 500 actions - NIS 119), at the end of this amount you will be charged NIS 30 again and you can perform another 500 actions (a total of 1000 actions - NIS 149), after that you will be charged NIS 149 for each 1000 actions more
(the amount of operations resets every month)
SeperateActoinsPriceExplanation:
In this route, you will be charged 35 NIS for 200 receipts, then you can choose to increase the package of withdraws or receipts according to the following specifications - 35 NIS for 500 receipts, then 28 NIS for another 500 receipts (a total of 200 clearings and 1000 receipts - 97 NIS) for every 1000 additional receipts will be charged an additional NIS 63. In addition, you can increase the withdraw package, for another 300 withdraws 35 NIS (a total of 500 withdraws 70 NIS) for another 500 withdraws 51.8 NIS (a total of 1000 withdraws 121.8 NIS) for each 1000 additional withdraws you will be charged an additional 121.8 NIS
(the amount of operations resets every month)

InvoicesOnlyExplanation:
In this route you will pay according to the amount of invoices. You will be charged 50 NIS in advance and you can issue 500 invoices, after that you will be charged a second time with 40 NIS and you will be entitled to another 500 invoices (a total of 1000) after that you will be charged 90 NIS for each additional 1000 invoices
 (the amount of receipts resets every a month)

unlimitedWindowsExplain: Customers will see all available slots without any restrictions. If this option is turned off, the number of slots displayed will be limited to the selected number
earliestExplain: The slots displayed to customers will start from the earliest slot, i.e. if the jump is 30 minutes and the shift starts at 8:00, the slots 8:00, 8:30, 9:00, etc. will be displayed. It is important to note that the number of slots displayed will be in accordance with the ״Maximum number of slots״ and the ״Slots limitation type״.
latestExplain: The slots displayed to customers will start from the latest slot, i.e. if the jump is 30 minutes and the shift ends at 16:00, the slots 15:30, 15:00, 14:30, etc. will be displayed. It is important to note that the number of slots displayed will be in accordance with the ״Maximum number of slots״  and the ״Slots limitation type״.
edgesExplain: The slots that will be displayed to customers will start from the latest slot and the earliest slots at the same time, meaning that if the jump is 30 minutes and the shift is 8:00 - 16:00, the slots 8:00, 8:30, 15:30, 15:00, etc. will be displayed. It is important to note that the amount of slots that will be displayed will be according to the ״Maximum number of slots״  and the ״Slots limitation type״.
perDayExplain: The number you set in ״Maximum number of slots״ will be per workday, for example if the maximum number of queues is 5, customers will only see the first five available slots in the order you selected in ״Order to display slots״
perShiftExplain: The number you set in ״Maximum number of slots״ will be per shift each day, for example if the maximum number of queues is 5 and you have 3 shifts today, customers will see up to 15 available slots, a maximum of the first five slots in each shift in the order you selected in ״Order to display slots״
durationSpaceExplanation: The time interval between available slots that will be displayed to customers, you can also set fixed times such as ״every half hour״ etc by activating ״Jump to fixed times only״, but this option may cause gaps in the schedule and is not recommended unless the business works according to fixed and non-dynamic meetings
slotsSettingsExplanation: If your business is growing and you don't want to allow customers to schedule appointments whenever they want, in order to prevent gaps, you can set a maximum number of slots that will be displayed to customers each day or shift. Only after a slot is taken, an additional slot will be displayed. The algorithm adheres to the beginning and end of the shift to prevent gaps, and you can change the ״Order to display slots״ according to the growth of the business.

openDocumentStatusInfo: Open document (draft) - a document that has not yet been issued as an official accounting document. It can be edited, changed, and deleted. It is not reportable to the authorities and is not considered income until it is closed.
closedDocumentStatusInfo: Closed document - an official accounting document issued according to law, with a sequential serial number. Once closed, it cannot be edited or deleted, and it is reported to the tax authorities and considered income for all purposes.
manuallyMarkedClosedDocumentStatusInfo: Manually closed document - a draft document that was approved and closed manually by the user. From that point, the document is considered closed for all purposes and cannot be edited.
cancelsOtherDocumentStatusInfo: Canceling document - an official accounting document, such as a credit invoice, created to cancel or correct another closed document. It is used to correct mistakes without changing the original document.
canceledDocumentStatusInfo: Canceled document - a closed document that was canceled by a canceling document, such as a credit invoice.
